generated_oml:
// C1: Enablers - Standards & Frameworks
instance ISO232471DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-1 (Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing) as a foundational reference for system architecture"
]

instance ISO30173DigitalTwinConcepts : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO/IEC 30173 (Digital Twin - Concepts and Terminology) for terminology alignment"
]

// C2: Hardware Platforms
instance NVIDIAJetsonNXPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX (embedded AI/ML accelerator for onboard processing)"
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 (Robot Operating System 2) as the middleware for real-time sensor fusion, AI pipelines, and communication"
]

// C3: Software Development Tools
instance Jetpack50SDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for hardware abstraction and AI model deployment"
]

instance Python38Environment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 for high-level scripting and data processing"
]

instance CPlusPlusEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ for low-level control and sensor interfacing"
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for simulation, prototyping, and AI model validation"
]

// C4: Data Management
instance SQLDatabaseSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for structured storage of vehicle telemetry, sensor logs, and DT metadata"
]

// C5: Edge Computing
instance LocalProcessingUnit : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Local processing on the Jetson NX for latency-sensitive tasks (e.g., ACC decision-making)"
]

instance CloudOffloadingSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Offloading to cloud-based DT infrastructure for non-critical analytics"
]

// C6: Simulation Engines
instance MATLABSimulinkIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration with MATLAB/Simulink for virtual testing of autonomous driving algorithms"
]

// C7: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensors integrated for high-precision environmental perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor state sensors for real-time motor control monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateTelemetry
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensors for vehicle control feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

instance ServoActuatorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo actuator state sensors for precise position control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state sensors (motor, steering, servo) for real-time tracking"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

// C8: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction - Data Types
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedData PointCloudData
]

instance MotorStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateData
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngle
]

instance ServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoActuatorStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPosition
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleState
]

instance PointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw 2D/3D LiDAR point coordinates from sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor speed and voltage commands from motor state sensors"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateTelemetry
]

instance SteeringAngle : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time steering angle measurements for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleData
]

instance ServoPosition : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Precise servo position data for steering and control systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ServoPositionData
]

instance VehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state including motor, steering, and servo parameters"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

instance ClusteringAndTrackingOutput : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks and projected driving paths from point cloud processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PointCloudData
]

instance ProcessedMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed metrics and vehicle state logs for visualization and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleState
]

// C9: Functional Entities
instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Data Pre-Processing Functional Entity for filtering and clustering raw point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ClusteringAndTrackingSoftware
    DTDFVocab:outputTo ClusteringAndTrackingOutput
]

instance ClusteringAndTrackingSoftware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component for point cloud clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingFE
    DTDFVocab:outputTo ClusteringAndTrackingOutput
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Data Translation Functional Entity for standardizing message types and unit conversions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudData, MotorStateData, SteeringAngle, ServoPosition
    DTDFVocab:outputTo PointCloudData, MotorStateTelemetry, SteeringAngleData, ServoPositionData
]

instance ActuationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Actuation Functional Entity for converting digital commands to physical actuation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorStateData, SteeringAngle, ServoPosition
    DTDFVocab:outputTo ElectronicSpeedController
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Presentation Functional Entity for formatting data for end-users (3D visualizations)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ClusteringAndTrackingOutput, VehicleState
    DTDFVocab:outputTo UserInterface
]

instance ReportGenerationFE : DTDFVocab:FunctionalEntity [
    base:desc "Report Generation Functional Entity for aggregating processed data into logs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleState
    DTDFVocab:outputTo SQLDatabaseSystem
]

// C10: Models/Data Relationships
instance VehicleGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation of the autonomous vehicle chassis geometry"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulinkIntegration
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral representation of autonomous driving algorithms and control strategies"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, MATLABSimulinkIntegration
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm model for real-time decision-making"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LocalProcessingUnit, CloudOffloadingSystem
]

// C11: Services
instance RealTimeDataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time streaming and visualization of sensor data (LiDAR, camera, ESC telemetry)"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC system computations from Jetson NX to cloud infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScalableAnalyticsAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Secure remote deployment, updates, and diagnostics via SQL database-backed APIs"
    DTDFVocab:provides FirmwareUpdateAction, ConfigurationManagementAction
]

// C12: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of vehicle state, environmental conditions, and system health"
]

instance ScalableAnalyticsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloading complex AI computations to cloud infrastructure for fault tolerance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FirmwareUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Secure remote deployment of ADAS firmware modules"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ConfigurationManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote configuration management of system parameters and settings"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C13: Physical Acting Components
instance ElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (Vedder) converting motor voltage commands to physical speed adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputFrom ActuationFE
]

instance ChassisControlUnit : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lower-level chassis control unit managing steering and speed adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:inputFrom ActuationFE
]

// C14: Physical Twin Components
instance AutonomousTraxxasChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10th-scale autonomous Traxxas vehicle chassis optimized for adaptive cruise control"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, MotorStateSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoActuatorStateSensor
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonPlatform : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson platform deployed for embedded AI processing"
    base:contains ROS2Middleware, LocalProcessingUnit
]

instance LiDARPerceptionSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "High-precision LiDAR sensor system for environmental perception"
    base:contains LiDARSensor
]

instance AutonomousControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Complete autonomous driving control system including sensors and actuators"
    base:contains ElectronicSpeedController, ChassisControlUnit, ActuationFE
]

// C15: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AutonomousTraxxasChassis, NVIDIAJetsonPlatform, LiDARPerceptionSystem, AutonomousControlSystem
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleGeometryModel, BehavioralModel, ACCAlgorithmModel, DataPreProcessingFE, DataTranslationFE
]

// C16: Communication Protocols
instance ROS2Communication : DTDFVocab:CommunicationProtocol [
    base:desc "ROS 2 topics/publishers for real-time data exchange between physical and digital components"
    DTDFVocab:connects ROS2Middleware, DataPreProcessingFE, DataTranslationFE, ActuationFE
]

instance WiFiUDPTransport : DTDFVocab:CommunicationProtocol [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi UDP transport layer for inter-entity communication (User ↔ DT)"
    DTDFVocab:connects PresentationFE, UserInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Control Flows from Digital to Physical:**
  - **Command Types:**
    - **Actuation Commands:** Motor/steering servo actuation (e.g., via Actuation FE or ACC Controlling FE).
    - **Data Signal Translation:** Digital commands (e.g., from ACC Controlling FE) translated into physical control signals (e.g., ROS2 → Wi-Fi UDP).
  - **Control Message Formats:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Standardizes message types (e.g., aligns actuation commands with physical hardware protocols).
    - **ROS2 Network:** Internal communication for command routing (e.g., ACC Controlling FE → Actuation FE).
  - **Validation & Authentication:**
    - **Implicit in Architecture:** No explicit mention of authentication (e.g., TLS, JWT) or command validation (e.g., safety checks).
    - **Feedback Systems:**
      - **Command Execution Confirmation:** Not explicitly documented (assumed implicit in ROS2/UDP acknowledgments).
    - **Conflict Resolution:**
      - **Not in Document:** Mechanisms for resolving digital-physical command conflicts (e.g., priority rules, fallback modes).
  - **Security:** No details on securing control signals (e.g., encryption, access controls).

  **Not in Document:** Transmission frequencies for control commands, explicit feedback loops (e.g., sensor feedback to digital twin), or real-time constraints.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **hardware-imposed clock synchronization with a strict 100 ms accuracy requirement** for time alignment between physical and digital components. This is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications where deviations beyond 100 ms would compromise real-time coordination, particularly for compute-offloading tasks. The system does not specify explicit synchronization protocols (e.g., NTP, PTP) but mandates adherence to standards mandating stricter synchronization than 100 ms, implying a **low-latency, deterministic synchronization framework** is employed to ensure temporal coherence across distributed hardware components (e.g., sensors, actuators, and ROS2 nodes)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)** for defining software components and their relationships, but introduces **deviations from the standard**:
  - **Architecture Pattern**: The system implements **Functional Entities (FEs)** as ROS2 packages, each hosting one or more ROS2 nodes to encapsulate functionality. This aligns with the ISO 23247 RA but extends it by **decomposing the User Interface FE into two subcomponents** (3D Visualization and Control Panel) and **expanding the Physical Twin Entity** to include general automotive functionalities (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) beyond resource-specific tasks (e.g., OME in the standard).
  - **Component Integration**: Components are mapped to hardware entities (e.g., sensors/actuators in the Physical Twin Entity, data management in the Digital Twin Entity) via ROS2’s **distributed middleware**, enabling cross-entity communication. The **entity-based structure** ensures ownership of functionalities is tied to hardware deployment, impacting the engineering process.
  - **Resource Management**: The system leverages **ROS2’s modularity** to dynamically deploy FE instances, but lacks explicit details on resource allocation (e.g., CPU/memory) or orchestration tools (e.g., Kubernetes). The **deviation from ISO 23247** implies a **customized orchestration approach** where the Physical Twin Entity’s scope is broader than the standard’s OME, enabling cross-cutting automotive features.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: Coordination is implicit through ROS2’s **topic/publisher-subscriber model** and entity-specific interfaces. No explicit mention of higher-level orchestration (e.g., service orchestration frameworks) or fault tolerance mechanisms is provided, but the **100 ms synchronization constraint** ensures deterministic timing for critical operations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly define **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for Digital Twin models. The **error detection and correction mechanisms** are not addressed in the documents, nor are **quality assurance processes** for models or data. While the standard emphasizes the Digital Twin Entity’s role in representing the OME, it lacks technical specifications for ensuring alignment between physical and digital states, such as error thresholds or iterative refinement protocols.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Network Infrastructure:**
  The Digital Twin system employs a **hybrid network architecture** combining onboard edge computing and centralized Wi-Fi communication:
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-Entity (Onboard):** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for low-latency, real-time communication between software components (e.g., LiDAR processing, actuation FE).
    - **Inter-Entity (Offboard):** Wi-Fi UDP for transmitting processed data (e.g., vehicle state, object tracks) to external servers.
  - **Network Topology:**
    - **Edge Layer:** Onboard components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) handle raw sensor processing (LiDAR clustering, motor/steering actuation).
    - **Centralized Backend:** Data is transmitted to external devices via Wi-Fi, with assumed redundancy for critical paths (e.g., redundant Wi-Fi links for LiDAR data).
  - **Bandwidth/Latency Requirements:**
    - **Latency:** At most **100ms** for critical data streams (e.g., LiDAR object tracks) to support autonomous driving functions (implied by R7 in documents).
    - **Bandwidth:** Assumed sufficient for LiDAR data (point clouds, clustering outputs) but not quantified in documents.
  - **Reliability/Redundancy:**
    - Redundant Wi-Fi links are assumed for critical paths (e.g., autonomous control feedback loops) to prevent data loss.
    - No explicit mention of failover mechanisms or network monitoring tools.
  - **Security Protocols:** Not specified in the documents; assumed absent or handled by higher-layer protocols (e.g., ROS2 security extensions not detailed).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  The Digital Twin system adopts a **hybrid deployment model** combining edge and remote hosting:
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Onboard components (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) handle real-time sensor processing (LiDAR clustering, motor/steering actuation).
    - **Remote Hosting:** Data storage and visualization occur on external devices (e.g., servers) connected via Wi-Fi, with no specific cloud/on-premise details provided.
  - **Computational/Storage Requirements:**
    - Edge: Low-power devices (e.g., Jetson Orin Nano) for lightweight processing (LiDAR clustering, actuation FE).
    - Remote: External storage for processed data (e.g., vehicle state logs, reports) but no specific capacity (e.g., petabytes/day) or cloud provider (e.g., AWS) mentioned.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly detailed; assumed to scale via distributed ROS2 networks or Wi-Fi load balancing (not quantified).
  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - Onboard edge deployment for sensor processing.
    - Offboard centralized storage for reports/data visualization.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - Onboard components assume high reliability (critical paths use redundant Wi-Fi).
    - No explicit SLA or uptime guarantees specified; assumed critical for autonomous driving applications.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  - **Relevant Standards**: The ISO 23247 – *Digital Twin framework for manufacturing* standard is mentioned, which defines a development framework for Digital Twins (DTs) in manufacturing. It is organized into four parts:
    1. Overview and general principles
    2. Reference architecture (RA) – Entity-based design with specific entities (e.g., Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity)
    3. Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements
    4. Information exchange

  - **Key Components of ISO 23247 (Part 2 – Reference Architecture)**:
    - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Any physical entity with observable presence/operation in manufacturing (e.g., equipment, facilities, products, personnel, processes).
    - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data collection from and control of the OME.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Represents the OME digitally.
    - **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, control).
    - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units handling specific tasks (e.g., actuation, data transformation, user interfacing). Implementation guidelines are not specified, but OPC Unified Architecture is suggested as a possible approach.

  - **Compliance and Interoperability**:
    - While ISO 23247 provides a foundational framework, no additional details on certification requirements, compliance frameworks, or interoperability standards beyond the standard itself are provided in the document.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin System (DTS) must adhere to **cybersecurity and safety protocols** relevant to **vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications** and **functional safety** in automotive applications. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols, the **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)** specifies **latency requirements** for V2X services, mandating a **maximum network latency of 100 ms** for real-time data exchange to ensure timely decision-making in autonomous driving scenarios.

For **safety-critical systems**, the DTS must comply with **ISO 23247:2023** (Road vehicles – Safety-related systems – Safety integrity requirements), which outlines **safety integrity levels (SIL)** and **assurance requirements** for safety-critical components. Additionally, the DTS must incorporate **fail-safe mechanisms** and **risk assessment approaches** as defined in **ISO/IEC 30173:2023** (Digital twin systems – Safety and security requirements), ensuring redundancy, fault tolerance, and compliance with **ISO 15622** (Intelligent Transport Systems – Adaptive Cruise Control performance requirements), which indirectly implies adherence to **functional safety standards** for autonomous driving systems.

No explicit mention of **authentication systems, access control mechanisms, or data anonymization** is present in the documents, thus these aspects remain unspecified."
]
